Welcome to Garland Pediatric Dental Clinic where kids have fun. The first time we will do an exam, cleaning and fluoride treatment. We will also take x-rays to detect cavities in between the teeth that are not noticeable in a visual examination.

Baby exams are important to assess the oral development, detect early signs of cavities, and to get all the information about how to maintain a good oral hygiene. You can bring your child as soon as he gets his first tooth which is usually around 6 months of age of earlier. Our Services Preventive: Dental exams for babies, children, teenagers, and special healthcare needs patients.

DO NOT send personal health information through this form. Specific patient care must be addressed during your appointment. Please complete the following form to request an appointment. Please also note that availability will vary depending on your request. Your appointment will be confirmed by phone by a member of our staff. Thank you. How Did You Hear About Us.

Pediatric Dentist in Garland, TX

--- New Patient Specials ---
New Patient Special $100 Exam, X-rays, cleaning. Free second opinions We offer: Personalized care Minimally invasive dental treatment options also known as a holistic or biological approach. We spend time with your child We try to avoid sedation Request an appointment

--- Appointments ---

--- Services ---
At home care and diet are very important to maintain excellent oral health since we only see you twice per year most of the time. Dental exams for babies, children, teenagers and special healthcare needs patients are important to prevent the need of future invasive procedures.

Digital X-rays Oral hygiene counseling Nutrition counseling Teeth cleaning Fluoride treatment Fluoride alternative treatment Arrest of caries, no drilling options, SIlver Diamine Fluoride Remineralization options Sealants BPA free. Sealants are a protective coat on all pit and fissures of posterior teeth that helps reduce plaque accumulation.

Restorative: Tooth colored fillings BPA free Pulp therapy LSTR pulp treatment Stainless steel crowns Tooth colored crowns Minimally invasive treatment options Sedation: We will evaluate the treatment needs of your child, and will discuss with you sedation options depending on your children needs and your personal preference. Nitrous oxide (laughing gas): to relax your child. He can go to back to regular activities after it. Oral sedation: your child will be conscious and may get drowsy.

If you have any questions or would like your children to be seen, don’t hesitate to contact us today at (214) 227-5227. --- Meet the Doctor ---
Dr. Carla Schmidl Dr Carla Schmidl is a Board Certified pediatric dentist by the American Board of Pediatric Dentistry. She has nitrous and oral sedation permits. /wp-content/uploads/2019/11/VID_20191109_170501. mp4 Our Story Dr. Schmidl did dental school and received her Dental Degree in Peru in 1998.

Deep sedation: in the office or at the hospital. We will discuss it with you. More: Dental trauma Extractions Athletic mouth guards to prevent avulsed or broken teeth when playing sports. Teeth whitening Removal of white or brown spots on teeth Early orthodontic evaluation: Space maintainer to preserve the space for permanent teeth.

All these together with a nutrition and oral hygiene counseling. The most important is to provide a relaxed experience free of anxiety for your child. Trust It is important to gain the trust of your child to obtain the best of him during the appointment. Our team will make every effort to make every visit to the dentist comfortable for your child. Fun Our office is kid oriented and has a fun environment.

Her desire to improve the quality of life of children took her to Boston to complete a 2 year specialty post doctoral degree in Pediatric Dentistry at Tufts University School of Dental Medicine. Dr. Schmidl will help you determine which is the most appropriate treatment option for your child by discussing with you all the options. As a mother Dr. Schmidl understands the concerns of parents when deciding about what is the best dental treatment for their child.
If the recommendations above do not meet your needs, we invite you to contact us for assistance.

It is our goal to ensure that each visit goes well so that they think of the dentist’s office as a good place. Our entire staff is wonderful with children, allowing them to relax while we care for their teeth. Our dentists and staff have received extra training for primary teeth. Our goal is to protect your children’s teeth so they can eat and speak correctly. We will do whatever we can to help your children have the bright smiles they deserve. We are also here for you, the parents.

She then did dental school and received her US Dental Degree from the University of the Pacific in San Francisco, California in 2009. Then she worked as a general dentist in the Dallas-Garland area where she enjoyed working with adults as well as children. After some years she moved to New York to do a 1 year residency that included training in special needs patients and hospital dentistry at the University of Rochester –Strong Memorial Hospital in Rochester, New York.

She will help you determine which is the most appropriate treatment option for your child by discussing with you all the options. Dr. Schmidl always tries to take a conservative approach when treatment planning. She enjoys traveling, spending time with her children and reading. --- About ---
Here at Garland Pediatric Dental Clinic, we are proud to specialize in pediatric dentistry. We truly believe in starting children off right when it comes to their dental health.

We know how important (yet difficult) it is to take care of your children’s teeth at home. During your appointment, we will go over the care that you need to be providing at home and look for problems as well. If needed, we will work with your child’s pediatrician. If you have any questions or would like your children to be seen, don’t hesitate to contact us today at (214) 227-5227. /wp-content/uploads/2019/11/VID_20191109_170501. mp4

All of our staff members are well trained and filled with compassion and understanding. They love working with children of all ages. We will do everything that we can to ensure that your child has a good visit.

This is a more natural and conservative dental treatment approach taking in consideration the overall health of your child not just his teeth. This is also known as a holistic or biological approach.
